questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the linked list data structure?
options: A) To store data in a single block of memory
B) To allow for efficient insertion and deletion of elements
C) To provide fast search capabilities
D) To sort data in ascending order
questions.explanation: Linked lists are particularly useful when the order of elements matters, and we need to frequently insert or delete elements from the list.

questions.question: Which of the following data structures is an example of a dynamic data structure?
questions.explanation: Dynamic data structures can change their size and structure during runtime, which is the case for linked lists.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using a binary search tree over a linear search?
questions.explanation: Binary search trees offer faster search times because the data is divided into two halves at each node, reducing the number of comparisons needed.

questions.question: Why is it important to choose the right data structure for a specific problem?
options: A) To make the code more efficient
questions.explanation: Choosing the right data structure can significantly impact the performance, scalability, and maintainability of a program.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between a queue and a stack?
options: A) A queue follows the FIFO principle, while a stack follows the LIFO principle
B) A queue is used for storing data, while a stack is used for retrieving data
C) A queue is used for sorting data, while a stack is used for searching data
D) A queue is used for parallel processing, while a stack is used for sequential processing
questions.explanation: A queue follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, where the first element that is added will be the first to be removed. A stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle.
